reasons: Based on the limited frontal view provided, an accurate Body Condition Score cannot be definitively determined as the ribs, waist, and abdominal tuck are not visible. From the visible face, neck, and upper chest, the dog does not appear overly emaciated or grossly obese. Assuming a healthy, proportionate body underneath the coat, an estimated score of 5 (Ideal) is assigned. In a true ideal state, ribs would be palpable without excess fat covering, a waist would be visible from above, and an abdominal tuck would be evident from the side.
